title: OpenAI Launches First Self-Developed AI Inference Chip, Boos... - moomoo Community
title_zh: OpenAI 发布首款自研 AI 推理芯片，性能提升...
full_text:
KallaShock Pan
wrote a post · Jun 26 10:01
Follow
OpenAI Launches First Self-Developed AI Inference Chip, Boosting NVIDIA/WiMi's Scale of Computing Power Advantage
It has been learned that on June 24, 2026 (local time), OpenAI and semiconductor giant Broadcom jointly released their first custom AI inference chip, Jalapeño.
First AI Inference Chip Unveiled
According to the disclosure, this is an application-specific integrated circuit (ASIC) designed specifically for large language model inference. In terms of division of labor, OpenAI is responsible for the underlying architecture design, Broadcom is responsible for silicon implementation and network hardware, and Canadian electronics manufacturing services provider Celestica is responsible for board and rack system integration.
OpenAI stated that Jalapeño's performance per watt will surpass current state-of-the-art levels. Currently, Jalapeño chip engineering samples are running various machine learning workloads in the laboratory at mass production target frequencies and power consumption, including GPT5.3, Codex, and Spark.
In its early stages, OpenAI primarily relied on exclusive partnerships with cloud providers, exchanging capital for computing power. It accepted investment from Microsoft (MSFT) and leased a dedicated Microsoft Azure cluster comprised of tens of thousands of NVIDIA GPUs for model training and hosting, optimizing high-speed communication between multiple chips.
OpenAI emphasizes that the world is moving towards a computing-centric economy. Jalapeño is part of OpenAI's long-term full-stack infrastructure strategy. The decision to launch Jalapeño in mid-2026 is a natural consequence of high operating costs, market competition, and supply chain pressures, enabling it to deliver more powerful intelligence with greater efficiency.
AI is reshaping global computing power competition. Currently, the demand for computing power from artificial intelligence (AI) is soaring. At NVIDIA's 2026 annual shareholder meeting on June 24th, CEO Jensen Huang emphasized that the "era of useful AI" has arrived and expressed confidence in continued AI infrastructure development.
Jensen Huang stated that every industry is vying to adopt Agentic AI, and the AI ​​industry can be imagined as a five-layered cake comprising energy, chips and systems, infrastructure, models, and applications. While traditional data centers provided storage and other services, current AI factories are used to create tokens, which explains the strong demand for computing power.
Huang also revealed the progress of NVIDIA's key products, stating that Hopper was previously built for pre-training, Blackwell enables rack-scale inference, and Vera Rubin is designed for intelligent agents; Vera Rubin is now in full production. Every major model developer, public cloud, AI cloud, and hyperscale cloud provider is preparing to build upon Vera Rubin.
Meanwhile, within the industry, Google (GOOG), with its self-developed TPU series chips, has demonstrated significant profit margins in computing power cost control and hardware-software synergy optimization. Anthropic has deeply integrated its capital and computing power with Amazon and Google, and is also collaborating with multiple computing power and hardware manufacturers to build a diversified computing power foundation.
WiMi's Open-Source Large-Model Computing Power Shows Signs of Scale Effect
WiMi, an AI vision innovation company, has built a full-chain investment model covering chip architecture design, cluster system optimization, and quantum AI chip R&D. Focusing on the technological synergy between AI chips and holographic AR and digital human businesses, it aims to unleash long-term synergistic effects and potentially gain a share of the AI ​​computing power wave.
For a long time, WiMi has been promoting the construction of AI chip clusters, focusing on the integration of computing power foundation and technology, exploring low-power chips and edge computing optimization, adapting to the low-latency requirements of vertical models such as embodied intelligence and multimodal computing, and improving AI cloud computing infrastructure to meet the computing power demands brought by generative AI and large models, continuously building industry solutions.
Furthermore, according to its 2025 annual report, WiMi achieved a net profit of 347 million yuan, a year-on-year surge of 235.9%. This net profit growth was mainly due to the rapid development of emerging businesses such as quantum technology, artificial intelligence, and holographic AR, demonstrating economies of scale and enhancing profitability. In the future, WiMi will continue to follow up on open-source large-model technology, optimizing algorithms to reduce application costs and accelerate the commercialization of scientific research results by opening up computing resources and technical interfaces. In conclusion, the competition in AI has essentially evolved into a competition for computing power. For OpenAI to maintain its technological and commercial leadership, it must complete the chip puzzle. OpenAI's multi-generational computing platform is planned for initial deployment by the end of 2026. However, it is crucial to be aware that whoever can first master the supply of low-cost, highly stable, and zero-carbon computing power will firmly grasp the core discourse power of the global AI industry and secure a key ticket to the AGI era.
$WiMi Hologram Cloud (WIMI.US)$ $NVIDIA (NVDA.US)$ $Alphabet-C (GOOG.US)$
